<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Simplicial sets and nerve</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>

  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[

        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},

    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>

Simplicial sets and nerve

Combinatorial models of spaces: $\Delta$, simplicial sets, geometric realization, and the nerve $N(C)$.

blocks.html:
1. The simplex category $\Delta$

The simplex category $\Delta$ is the category whose objects are the finite non-empty totally ordered sets $[n] = \{0 < 1 < \cdots < n\}$ and whose morphisms $[m] \to [n]$ are weakly order-preserving maps. It is small (a skeleton of the category of finite non-empty totally ordered sets) and combinatorial: the entire structure is captured by two families of generating morphisms.

The cofaces $d^i\colon [n-1] \to [n]$ ($0 \le i \le n$) are the unique injective order-preserving maps that miss the value $i$; they correspond geometrically to the $i$-th face inclusion of the standard $n$-simplex. The codegeneracies $s^j\colon [n+1] \to [n]$ ($0 \le j \le n$) are the unique surjective order-preserving maps that double the value $j$; they collapse two adjacent vertices into one. Every morphism in $\Delta$ factors uniquely as a composite of codegeneracies followed by cofaces, and the relations among the generators are the cosimplicial identities:

$d^j d^i = d^i d^{j-1}\;(i j+1.\end{cases}$

The morphism count is a useful sanity check: order-preserving maps $[m] \to [n]$ correspond to weakly increasing sequences of length $m+1$ in $\{0,\ldots,n\}$, equivalently multisets of size $m+1$ from $n+1$ symbols, giving $\binom{m+n+1}{m+1}$. So $\Delta([1],[3]) = \binom{5}{2} = 10$. Two of these are degenerate (the constants); the rest split into compositions of the four cofaces and the lone $s^0\colon [1]\to[0]$ — which, taken together with the cosimplicial identities, is enough to generate $\Delta$.

The reason this matters: a presheaf on $\Delta$ — a contravariant functor $\Delta^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathbf{Set}$ — is exactly determined by sets $X_n$ together with the dualised generators $d_i\colon X_n \to X_{n-1}$ and $s_j\colon X_n \to X_{n+1}$ satisfying the dual simplicial identities. That is the data of a simplicial set, the subject of §2.

See also
  • Categories and morphisms
  • Sets and functions

Used in

<script>
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById('svg-delta-generators');
  const out = document.getElementById('delta-readout');
  const reset = document.getElementById('delta-reset');
  if(!svg) return;
  // four columns: [0], [1], [2], [3]
  const COLS = [
    { name:'[0]', x: 80,  vertices:[140] },
    { name:'[1]', x: 220, vertices:[100, 200] },
    { name:'[2]', x: 360, vertices:[ 60, 140, 220] },
    { name:'[3]', x: 500, vertices:[ 30, 100, 170, 240] },
  ];
  // Generators rendered as arrows between adjacent columns, with explanation.
  // Cofaces d^i: [n] -> [n+1] inject and miss value i. Codegeneracies s^j: [n+1] -> [n] surject doubling j.
  const GENS = [
    // [0] -> [1]: d^0 hits 1, d^1 hits 0.
    { id:'d0_01', kind:'face',   src:0, tgt:1, label:'d⁰',
      desc:'d⁰ : [0] → [1] sends 0 ↦ 1 — the inclusion missing vertex 0.', srcMap:[140], tgtPick:[200] },
    { id:'d1_01', kind:'face',   src:0, tgt:1, label:'d¹',
      desc:'d¹ : [0] → [1] sends 0 ↦ 0 — the inclusion missing vertex 1.', srcMap:[140], tgtPick:[100], offset:18 },
    { id:'s0_10', kind:'degen',  src:1, tgt:0, label:'s⁰',
      desc:'s⁰ : [1] → [0] is the unique surjection — collapses both vertices to 0.' },
    // [1] -> [2]: three cofaces.
    { id:'d0_12', kind:'face',   src:1, tgt:2, label:'d⁰',
      desc:'d⁰ : [1] → [2] sends (0,1) ↦ (1,2) — inclusion missing vertex 0.' },
    { id:'d1_12', kind:'face',   src:1, tgt:2, label:'d¹',
      desc:'d¹ : [1] → [2] sends (0,1) ↦ (0,2) — inclusion missing vertex 1.', offset:18 },
    { id:'d2_12', kind:'face',   src:1, tgt:2, label:'d²',
      desc:'d² : [1] → [2] sends (0,1) ↦ (0,1) — inclusion missing vertex 2.', offset:36 },
    // [2] -> [1]: two codegens.
    { id:'s0_21', kind:'degen',  src:2, tgt:1, label:'s⁰',
      desc:'s⁰ : [2] → [1] sends (0,1,2) ↦ (0,0,1) — doubles vertex 0.' },
    { id:'s1_21', kind:'degen',  src:2, tgt:1, label:'s¹',
      desc:'s¹ : [2] → [1] sends (0,1,2) ↦ (0,1,1) — doubles vertex 1.', offset:18 },
    // [2] -> [3]: a sample coface.
    { id:'d1_23', kind:'face',   src:2, tgt:3, label:'d¹',
      desc:'d¹ : [2] → [3] sends (0,1,2) ↦ (0,2,3) — inclusion missing vertex 1.' },
    { id:'s0_32', kind:'degen',  src:3, tgt:2, label:'s⁰',
      desc:'s⁰ : [3] → [2] sends (0,1,2,3) ↦ (0,0,1,2) — doubles vertex 0.' },
  ];
  const NS = 'http://www.w3.org/2000/svg';
  function el(t,a){ const e=document.createElementNS(NS,t); for(const k in a) e.setAttribute(k,a[k]); return e; }
  let active = null;

  function drawCols(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    // defs marker
    const defs = el('defs');
    const mk = el('marker',{id:'arr-delta', viewBox:'0 0 10 10', refX:9, refY:5, markerWidth:6, markerHeight:6, orient:'auto'});
    mk.appendChild(el('path',{d:'M0,0 L10,5 L0,10 z', fill:'var(--blue)'}));
    defs.appendChild(mk);
    const mkY = el('marker',{id:'arr-delta-y', viewBox:'0 0 10 10', refX:9, refY:5, markerWidth:6, markerHeight:6, orient:'auto'});
    mkY.appendChild(el('path',{d:'M0,0 L10,5 L0,10 z', fill:'var(--yellow)'}));
    defs.appendChild(mkY);
    svg.appendChild(defs);

    // column labels and vertices
    for(const c of COLS){
      const t = el('text',{x:c.x, y:14, 'text-anchor':'middle', 'font-size':13, fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent = c.name; svg.appendChild(t);
      c.vertices.forEach((y,i)=>{
        svg.appendChild(el('circle',{cx:c.x, cy:y, r:4, fill:'var(--ink)'}));
        const lab = el('text',{x:c.x+10, y:y+4, 'font-size':11, fill:'var(--mute)'});
        lab.textContent = String(i); svg.appendChild(lab);
      });
    }
    // arrows
    for(const g of GENS){
      const c1 = COLS[g.src], c2 = COLS[g.tgt];
      const y1 = (c1.vertices.reduce((a,b)=>a+b,0)/c1.vertices.length);
      const y2 = (c2.vertices.reduce((a,b)=>a+b,0)/c2.vertices.length);
      const dir = c2.x > c1.x ? 1 : -1;
      const off = (g.offset||0) * (g.kind==='face' ? -1 : 1);
      const x1 = c1.x + dir*22, x2 = c2.x - dir*22;
      const my = (y1+y2)/2 + off;
      const color = (active===g.id) ? 'var(--yellow)' : (g.kind==='face' ? 'var(--blue)' : 'var(--green)');
      const markerId = (active===g.id) ? 'arr-delta-y' : 'arr-delta';
      const pathStr = `M${x1} ${y1} Q ${(x1+x2)/2} ${my} ${x2} ${y2}`;
      const p = el('path',{d:pathStr, fill:'none', stroke:color, 'stroke-width':1.7, 'marker-end':`url(#${markerId})`});
      svg.appendChild(p);
      // label
      const lt = el('text',{x:(x1+x2)/2, y:my-6, 'text-anchor':'middle', 'font-size':11,
        fill:color, 'font-style':'italic'});
      lt.textContent = g.label;
      svg.appendChild(lt);
      // hit-rect for click
      const r = el('rect',{x:Math.min(x1,x2)-2, y:Math.min(y1,y2,my)-12,
        width:Math.abs(x2-x1)+4, height:Math.abs(y2-y1)+Math.abs(off)+24,
        fill:'transparent', style:'cursor:pointer', 'data-gid':g.id});
      svg.appendChild(r);
    }
    // legend
    const lg = el('g',{transform:'translate(20, 252)'});
    const lt1 = el('text',{x:0,y:0,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--blue)'});
    lt1.textContent = 'blue: cofaces dⁱ (inject, miss i)'; lg.appendChild(lt1);
    const lt2 = el('text',{x:0,y:16,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--green)'});
    lt2.textContent = 'green: codegens sⁱ (surject, double i)'; lg.appendChild(lt2);
    svg.appendChild(lg);
  }

  function showState(){
    if(!active){ out.textContent = 'click a generating morphism…'; return; }
    const g = GENS.find(x=>x.id===active);
    if(!g) return;
    const tag = g.kind==='face' ? 'COFACE' : 'CODEGENERACY';
    let extra = '';
    if(g.id==='d0_12') extra = '\n\nrelation: dⁱdʲ = dʲdⁱ⁻¹ for i<j (inserting two missing vertices is order-independent)';
    if(g.id==='d2_12') extra = '\n\nthe simplex with vertices (0,1) sits as the bottom face of [2] when we miss vertex 2';
    if(g.id==='s0_21') extra = '\n\nrelation: sⁱsʲ = sʲsⁱ⁺¹ for i≤j (doubling twice with adjacent indices commutes after a shift)';
    if(g.id==='s1_21') extra = '\n\nthe pair s⁰, s¹ : [2]→[1] are exactly the two ways to be the surjection [2]↠[1] in Δ';
    if(g.id==='d1_23') extra = '\n\nrelation: sʲdⁱ = dⁱ⁻¹sʲ for i > j+1; with i=1, j=0 here: s⁰d¹ = d¹s⁰? not quite, check the third identity.';
    out.textContent = `${tag}: ${g.desc}${extra}`;
  }

  svg.addEventListener('click', (e)=>{
    const gid = e.target && e.target.getAttribute && e.target.getAttribute('data-gid');
    if(gid){ active = gid; drawCols(); showState(); }
  });
  reset.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ active=null; drawCols(); showState(); });

  drawCols(); showState();
})();
</script>

blocks.html:
2. Simplicial sets as $\Delta^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathbf{Set}$

A simplicial set is a presheaf on $\Delta$, i.e. a functor $X\colon \Delta^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathbf{Set}$. Concretely it is the data of a sequence of sets $X_0, X_1, X_2, \ldots$ (the "$n$-simplices" $X_n$) together with face operators $d_i\colon X_n \to X_{n-1}$ ($0 \le i \le n$) and degeneracy operators $s_j\colon X_n \to X_{n+1}$ ($0 \le j \le n$) satisfying the (dualised) simplicial identities — the relations dual to those of §1. Maps of simplicial sets are natural transformations, equivalently graded maps respecting all $d_i, s_j$. The category $\mathbf{sSet} = \mathbf{Set}^{\Delta^{\mathrm{op}}}$ inherits a presheaf topos structure: it has all small limits and colimits, exponentials, and a subobject classifier.

The motivating examples are the standard simplices $\Delta^n = \Delta(-, [n])\colon [m] \mapsto \Delta([m],[n])$ — the representable presheaves. By Yoneda, maps $\Delta^n \to X$ are exactly the elements of $X_n$, so each $n$-simplex $\sigma \in X_n$ is a map $\sigma\colon \Delta^n \to X$. Faces and degeneracies of $\sigma$ are the precompositions of $\sigma$ with the cofaces and codegeneracies of $\Delta$.

The boundary $\partial \Delta^n \subset \Delta^n$ is the subobject consisting of all simplices that factor through some $d^i\colon \Delta^{n-1} \to \Delta^n$ — the union of the $n+1$ codimension-$1$ faces. The horn $\Lambda^n_k \subset \partial \Delta^n$ is the further subobject obtained by removing the $k$-th face: it is the union of all faces except the one opposite vertex $k$. The horn is inner when $0 < k < n$ and outer otherwise.

A useful structural fact: every simplex factors uniquely as a degeneracy of a non-degenerate one (the Eilenberg–Zilber lemma). For $\sigma \in X_n$ there exist a unique surjection $\alpha\colon [n] \twoheadrightarrow [k]$ in $\Delta$ and a unique non-degenerate $\tau \in X_k$ with $\sigma = X(\alpha)(\tau)$. This is what eventually lets the geometric realization of §3 land in CW-complexes — one cell per non-degenerate simplex.

See also
  • Presheaf toposes $\hat{C} = [C^{\mathrm{op}}, \mathbf{Set}]$

<script>
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById('svg-sset-faces');
  const out = document.getElementById('sset-readout');
  if(!svg) return;
  const NS = 'http://www.w3.org/2000/svg';
  function el(t,a){ const e=document.createElementNS(NS,t); for(const k in a) e.setAttribute(k,a[k]); return e; }

  // Vertices of Δ² laid out as a triangle, edges labeled (01), (02), (12), interior the unique 2-simplex.
  const V = [ {id:'v0', x:280, y:60,  label:'(0)'},
              {id:'v1', x:120, y:240, label:'(1)'},
              {id:'v2', x:440, y:240, label:'(2)'} ];
  const E = [
    {id:'e01', a:0, b:1, label:'(0,1)', faces:'d₀(0,1)=(1), d₁(0,1)=(0); degens s₀(0,1)=(0,0,1), s₁(0,1)=(0,1,1).'},
    {id:'e02', a:0, b:2, label:'(0,2)', faces:'d₀(0,2)=(2), d₁(0,2)=(0); degens s₀(0,2)=(0,0,2), s₁(0,2)=(0,2,2).'},
    {id:'e12', a:1, b:2, label:'(1,2)', faces:'d₀(1,2)=(2), d₁(1,2)=(1); degens s₀(1,2)=(1,1,2), s₁(1,2)=(1,2,2).'}
  ];
  const T = { id:'t012', label:'(0,1,2)',
              faces:'d₀(0,1,2)=(1,2), d₁(0,1,2)=(0,2), d₂(0,1,2)=(0,1).' };

  let active = null;
  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    // shaded interior
    const tri = el('polygon',{points:V.map(v=>`${v.x},${v.y}`).join(' '),
      fill: active==='t012' ? 'color-mix(in srgb, var(--yellow) 28%, var(--panel2))'
                              : 'color-mix(in srgb, var(--violet) 18%, var(--panel2))',
      stroke:'var(--violet)', 'stroke-width':1.2,
      style:'cursor:pointer', 'data-id':'t012'});
    svg.appendChild(tri);
    // edges
    for(const e of E){
      const a=V[e.a], b=V[e.b];
      const stroke = active===e.id ? 'var(--yellow)' : 'var(--blue)';
      const w = active===e.id ? 3.4 : 2.2;
      svg.appendChild(el('line',{x1:a.x,y1:a.y,x2:b.x,y2:b.y, stroke, 'stroke-width':w,
        style:'cursor:pointer','data-id':e.id}));
      // edge label
      const mx=(a.x+b.x)/2, my=(a.y+b.y)/2;
      const lab = el('text',{x:mx, y:my-8, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11, fill:'var(--mute)'});
      lab.textContent = e.label; svg.appendChild(lab);
    }
    // vertices
    for(const v of V){
      const fill = active===v.id ? 'var(--yellow)' : 'var(--ink)';
      svg.appendChild(el('circle',{cx:v.x, cy:v.y, r:6, fill, style:'cursor:pointer','data-id':v.id}));
      const t = el('text',{x:v.x, y:v.y - 12, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12, fill:'var(--ink)'});
      t.textContent = v.label; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // top-2-simplex label
    const cx = (V[0].x+V[1].x+V[2].x)/3, cy = (V[0].y+V[1].y+V[2].y)/3;
    const tlab = el('text',{x:cx, y:cy+5, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':13,
      fill:'var(--ink)','font-style':'italic'});
    tlab.textContent = T.label; svg.appendChild(tlab);
  }
  function showState(){
    if(!active){ out.textContent = 'click a vertex, edge, or the interior of the triangle…'; return; }
    if(active.startsWith('v')){
      const v = V.find(x=>x.id===active);
      out.textContent = `0-SIMPLEX ${v.label} — no faces; s₀${v.label} = ${v.label.replace(')','')},${v.label.slice(1)} (the constant edge).`;
    } else if(active.startsWith('e')){
      const e = E.find(x=>x.id===active);
      out.textContent = `1-SIMPLEX ${e.label}\n${e.faces}`;
    } else {
      out.textContent = `2-SIMPLEX (0,1,2) — the unique non-degenerate 2-simplex of Δ².\n${T.faces}\n\nDegens: s₀ doubles vertex 0, s₁ doubles 1, s₂ doubles 2 — three distinct 3-simplices in (Δ²)₃, plus more iterated degens of (0,1,2) for higher n.`;
    }
  }
  svg.addEventListener('click', (e)=>{
    const id = e.target && e.target.getAttribute && e.target.getAttribute('data-id');
    if(id){ active = id; draw(); showState(); }
  });
  draw(); showState();
})();
</script>

blocks.html:
3. Geometric realization $|X|$

The geometric realization functor $|{-}|\colon \mathbf{sSet} \to \mathbf{Top}$ is the left Kan extension of the cosimplicial space $[n] \mapsto |\Delta^n|$ (the standard topological $n$-simplex) along the Yoneda embedding $\Delta \hookrightarrow \mathbf{sSet}$. Concretely:

$|X| \;=\; \Bigl( \coprod_{n \ge 0} X_n \times |\Delta^n| \Bigr) \,\Big/\, \sim,$

where the equivalence relation glues $(\sigma, \alpha_*(t)) \sim (\alpha^*\sigma, t)$ for every $\alpha\colon [m] \to [n]$ in $\Delta$, every $\sigma \in X_n$, and every $t \in |\Delta^m|$. This is exactly the colimit over the category of simplices of $X$ of the diagram sending each simplex to its standard topological simplex.

Geometric realization is the left adjoint to the singular complex functor $\mathrm{Sing}\colon \mathbf{Top} \to \mathbf{sSet}$ defined by $\mathrm{Sing}(Y)_n = \mathrm{Top}(|\Delta^n|, Y)$. The adjunction $|{-}| \dashv \mathrm{Sing}$ is the bedrock of the simplicial approach to homotopy theory. Quillen's theorem upgrades it to a Quillen equivalence of model categories, so the homotopy theories of Kan complexes and topological spaces (with weak equivalences) agree.

Three computations make the picture concrete: $|\Delta^n|$ is the standard $n$-simplex; $|\partial \Delta^n| \cong S^{n-1}$ (its boundary is the $(n-1)$-sphere); and $|\Lambda^n_k|$ is the union of all faces but one — for $n=2$ this is two segments meeting at a vertex, contractible. Because of the Eilenberg–Zilber lemma, $|X|$ has a canonical CW-structure with one $n$-cell per non-degenerate $n$-simplex of $X$. Walk through the construction step by step below.

params.bodyScript:
<script>
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById('svg-realization');
  const out = document.getElementById('real-readout');
  const prev = document.getElementById('real-prev');
  const next = document.getElementById('real-next');
  const counter = document.getElementById('real-counter');
  if(!svg) return;
  const NS = 'http://www.w3.org/2000/svg';
  function el(t,a){ const e=document.createElementNS(NS,t); for(const k in a) e.setAttribute(k,a[k]); return e; }

  // Triangle vertices
  const V = [{x:280,y:60},{x:120,y:240},{x:440,y:240}];

  let step = 0;
  const STEPS = [
    { title: 'STEP 1 / 5 — start with the disjoint coproduct',
      desc: 'For Δ² we have non-degenerate simplices: 3 vertices in dim 0, 3 edges in dim 1, 1 triangle in dim 2. Form the disjoint union of the corresponding topological simplices: three points, three closed intervals, and one filled triangle.',
      draw: ()=>{
        // three isolated points (left), three intervals (middle), one triangle (right)
        for(let i=0;i<3;i++) svg.appendChild(el('circle',{cx:60+18*i, cy:80, r:5, fill:'var(--blue)'}));
        for(let i=0;i<3;i++){
          svg.appendChild(el('line',{x1:50, y1:140+22*i, x2:130, y2:140+22*i,
            stroke:'var(--green)','stroke-width':3}));
          svg.appendChild(el('circle',{cx:50, cy:140+22*i, r:3, fill:'var(--ink)'}));
          svg.appendChild(el('circle',{cx:130, cy:140+22*i, r:3, fill:'var(--ink)'}));
        }
        // floating triangle
        svg.appendChild(el('polygon',{points:'400,80 350,200 460,200',
          fill:'color-mix(in srgb, var(--violet) 28%, var(--panel2))', stroke:'var(--violet)','stroke-width':1.4}));
        const t = el('text',{x:280,y:24,'font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)','text-anchor':'middle'});
        t.textContent = '∐ Xₙ × |Δⁿ| — disjoint coproduct, no gluing yet'; svg.appendChild(t);
      } },
    { title: 'STEP 2 / 5 — glue via face inclusions',
      desc: 'For each face map d_i: X_n → X_{n-1} we identify (σ, d_i(t)) ∈ X_n × |Δⁿ| with (d_i σ, t) ∈ X_{n-1} × |Δ^{n-1}|. Effect: the three edges glue to specific vertices via their endpoints.',
      draw: ()=>{
        // edges + vertices in their target positions: 3 edges form an outer triangle of the eventual realization
        // place vertices at triangle corners, edges between them
        const A=V[0], B=V[1], C=V[2];
        svg.appendChild(el('line',{x1:A.x,y1:A.y,x2:B.x,y2:B.y, stroke:'var(--green)','stroke-width':3}));
        svg.appendChild(el('line',{x1:A.x,y1:A.y,x2:C.x,y2:C.y, stroke:'var(--green)','stroke-width':3}));
        svg.appendChild(el('line',{x1:B.x,y1:B.y,x2:C.x,y2:C.y, stroke:'var(--green)','stroke-width':3}));
        for(const v of V) svg.appendChild(el('circle',{cx:v.x,cy:v.y,r:6,fill:'var(--blue)'}));
        const t = el('text',{x:280,y:24,'font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)','text-anchor':'middle'});
        t.textContent = 'edges glue to vertices via face maps — boundary ∂Δ² takes shape';
        svg.appendChild(t);
      } },
    { title: 'STEP 3 / 5 — this is |∂Δ²|',
      desc: 'After all face-map gluings (and we still have NO 2-cell attached), what we have is the realization of the boundary subobject ∂Δ² ⊂ Δ². Topologically it is the triangle perimeter — a circle S¹.',
      draw: ()=>{
        const A=V[0], B=V[1], C=V[2];
        svg.appendChild(el('line',{x1:A.x,y1:A.y,x2:B.x,y2:B.y, stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':4}));
        svg.appendChild(el('line',{x1:A.x,y1:A.y,x2:C.x,y2:C.y, stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':4}));
        svg.appendChild(el('line',{x1:B.x,y1:B.y,x2:C.x,y2:C.y, stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':4}));
        for(const v of V) svg.appendChild(el('circle',{cx:v.x,cy:v.y,r:6,fill:'var(--blue)'}));
        const t = el('text',{x:280,y:24,'font-size':13,fill:'var(--cyan)','text-anchor':'middle'});
        t.textContent = '|∂Δ²| ≅ S¹'; svg.appendChild(t);
      } },
    { title: 'STEP 4 / 5 — attach the 2-cell',
      desc: 'The unique non-degenerate 2-simplex (0,1,2) ∈ (Δ²)₂ contributes a copy of |Δ²|. Its three edges are glued to the three edges of |∂Δ²| along d₀, d₁, d₂. Result: the disk filled in.',
      draw: ()=>{
        const A=V[0], B=V[1], C=V[2];
        svg.appendChild(el('polygon',{points:`${A.x},${A.y} ${B.x},${B.y} ${C.x},${C.y}`,
          fill:'color-mix(in srgb, var(--violet) 30%, var(--panel2))', stroke:'var(--violet)','stroke-width':2}));
        for(const v of V) svg.appendChild(el('circle',{cx:v.x,cy:v.y,r:6,fill:'var(--blue)'}));
        const t = el('text',{x:280,y:24,'font-size':13,fill:'var(--violet)','text-anchor':'middle'});
        t.textContent = '|Δ²| ≅ D² (the closed 2-disk)'; svg.appendChild(t);
      } },
    { title: 'STEP 5 / 5 — degenerate simplices contribute nothing new',
      desc: 'Eilenberg–Zilber: every degenerate simplex factors through a non-degenerate one via a surjection α. The relation (σ, α_*(t)) ∼ (α^*σ, t) collapses the entire copy of |Δⁿ| above a degenerate σ onto a copy of a lower-dim |Δᵏ|. The CW structure has exactly one cell per NON-degenerate simplex.',
      draw: ()=>{
        const A=V[0], B=V[1], C=V[2];
        svg.appendChild(el('polygon',{points:`${A.x},${A.y} ${B.x},${B.y} ${C.x},${C.y}`,
          fill:'color-mix(in srgb, var(--violet) 30%, var(--panel2))', stroke:'var(--violet)','stroke-width':2}));
        for(const v of V) svg.appendChild(el('circle',{cx:v.x,cy:v.y,r:6,fill:'var(--blue)'}));
        // annotate cells
        const lab = el('text',{x:280,y:165,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--ink)','text-anchor':'middle'});
        lab.textContent = '1 two-cell, 3 one-cells, 3 zero-cells'; svg.appendChild(lab);
        const t = el('text',{x:280,y:24,'font-size':13,fill:'var(--green)','text-anchor':'middle'});
        t.textContent = 'CW-complex: one cell per non-degenerate simplex'; svg.appendChild(t);
      } }
  ];
  function render(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    STEPS[step].draw();
    counter.textContent = `step ${step+1} / ${STEPS.length}`;
    out.textContent = STEPS[step].title + '\n\n' + STEPS[step].desc;
    prev.disabled = step===0;
    next.disabled = step===STEPS.length-1;
  }
  prev.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ if(step>0){ step--; render(); }});
  next.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ if(step<STEPS.length-1){ step++; render(); }});
  render();
})();
</script>

        svg.appendChild(el('polygon',{points:`${A.x},${A.y} ${B.x},${B.y} ${C.x},${C.y}`,
          fill:'color-mix(in srgb, var(--violet) 30%, var(--panel2))', stroke:'var(--violet)','stroke-width':2}));
        for(const v of V) svg.appendChild(el('circle',{cx:v.x,cy:v.y,r:6,fill:'var(--blue)'}));
        const t = el('text',{x:280,y:24,'font-size':13,fill:'var(--violet)','text-anchor':'middle'});
        t.textContent = '|Δ²| ≅ D² (the closed 2-disk)'; svg.appendChild(t);
      } },
    { title: 'STEP 5 / 5 — degenerate simplices contribute nothing new',
      desc: 'Eilenberg–Zilber: every degenerate simplex factors through a non-degenerate one via a surjection α. The relation (σ, α_*(t)) ∼ (α^*σ, t) collapses the entire copy of |Δⁿ| above a degenerate σ onto a copy of a lower-dim |Δᵏ|. The CW structure has exactly one cell per NON-degenerate simplex.',
      draw: ()=>{
        const A=V[0], B=V[1], C=V[2];
        svg.appendChild(el('polygon',{points:`${A.x},${A.y} ${B.x},${B.y} ${C.x},${C.y}`,
          fill:'color-mix(in srgb, var(--violet) 30%, var(--panel2))', stroke:'var(--violet)','stroke-width':2}));
        for(const v of V) svg.appendChild(el('circle',{cx:v.x,cy:v.y,r:6,fill:'var(--blue)'}));
        // annotate cells
        const lab = el('text',{x:280,y:165,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--ink)','text-anchor':'middle'});
        lab.textContent = '1 two-cell, 3 one-cells, 3 zero-cells'; svg.appendChild(lab);
        const t = el('text',{x:280,y:24,'font-size':13,fill:'var(--green)','text-anchor':'middle'});
        t.textContent = 'CW-complex: one cell per non-degenerate simplex'; svg.appendChild(t);
      } }
  ];
  function render(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    STEPS[step].draw();
    counter.textContent = `step ${step+1} / ${STEPS.length}`;
    out.textContent = STEPS[step].title + '\n\n' + STEPS[step].desc;
    prev.disabled = step===0;
    next.disabled = step===STEPS.length-1;
  }
  prev.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ if(step>0){ step--; render(); }});
  next.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ if(step<STEPS.length-1){ step++; render(); }});
  render();
})();
</script>

See also

  • Topological spaces

4. The nerve $N(C)$ of a category

For a small category $C$, the nerve $N(C)$ is the simplicial set with

$N(C)_n \;=\; \mathbf{Cat}([n], C) \;=\; \{\, x_0 \xrightarrow{f_1} x_1 \xrightarrow{f_2} \cdots \xrightarrow{f_n} x_n \,\}$,

the set of length-$n$ composable strings of arrows in $C$ (where we view $[n]$ as the linear poset $0 < 1 < \cdots < n$, hence as a category with a unique arrow $i \to j$ whenever $i \le j$). The face maps $d_i$ act on $(f_1, \ldots, f_n)$ as: $d_0$ drops $f_1$, $d_n$ drops $f_n$, and for $0 < i < n$ the operator $d_i$ composes $f_{i+1} \circ f_i$. The degeneracies $s_j$ insert an identity at position $j$.

This bridges category theory and homotopy theory: $N\colon \mathbf{Cat} \to \mathbf{sSet}$ is a fully faithful right adjoint, with left adjoint $h$ — the homotopy / fundamental category functor that sends a simplicial set $X$ to the category with objects $X_0$, generating arrows $X_1$, and relations $d_1 \sigma = d_0 \sigma \circ d_2 \sigma$ for $\sigma \in X_2$. Because $N$ is fully faithful, two small categories are equivalent iff their nerves are equivalent in the Joyal model structure on $\mathbf{sSet}$.

Two computations to keep in mind. First, for a poset $P$ viewed as a category, $N(P)_n$ is the set of weakly increasing length-$(n+1)$ chains in $P$. Second, for a group $G$ regarded as a one-object groupoid $BG$, $N(BG)_n = G^n$ — the standard simplicial model for the classifying space, with $|N(BG)| \simeq BG$ a $K(G,1)$.

<script>
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById('svg-nerve-cat');
  const out = document.getElementById('nerve-readout');
  const reset = document.getElementById('nerve-reset');
  if(!svg) return;
  const NS = 'http://www.w3.org/2000/svg';
  function el(t,a){ const e=document.createElementNS(NS,t); for(const k in a) e.setAttribute(k,a[k]); return e; }

  // C: three objects A,B,C with f:A→B, g:B→C, h=g∘f:A→C, plus identities (implicit)
  const OBJ = { A:{x:120, y:200, label:'A'}, B:{x:280, y:60,  label:'B'}, C:{x:440, y:200, label:'C'} };
  const ARR = [
    {id:'f', src:'A', tgt:'B', label:'f', curve:18},
    {id:'g', src:'B', tgt:'C', label:'g', curve:18},
    {id:'h', src:'A', tgt:'C', label:'h = g∘f', curve:30}
  ];
  let picks = []; // sequence of arrow ids

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const defs = el('defs');
    for(const c of ['blue','yellow','mute']){
      const m = el('marker',{id:'arr-nerve-'+c, viewBox:'0 0 10 10', refX:9, refY:5, markerWidth:6, markerHeight:6, orient:'auto'});
      m.appendChild(el('path',{d:'M0,0 L10,5 L0,10 z', fill:`var(--${c})`}));
      defs.appendChild(m);
    }
    svg.appendChild(defs);
    // arrows
    for(const a of ARR){
      const s = OBJ[a.src], t = OBJ[a.tgt];
      const dx = t.x-s.x, dy = t.y-s.y, L = Math.hypot(dx,dy);
      const ux = dx/L, uy = dy/L;
      const x1 = s.x + ux*22, y1 = s.y + uy*22;
      const x2 = t.x - ux*22, y2 = t.y - uy*22;
      const mx = (x1+x2)/2 - uy*a.curve, my = (y1+y2)/2 + ux*a.curve;
      let color = 'var(--blue)', mid = 'arr-nerve-blue';
      if(picks.includes(a.id)){ color='var(--yellow)'; mid='arr-nerve-yellow'; }
      const path = el('path',{d:`M${x1} ${y1} Q ${mx} ${my} ${x2} ${y2}`, fill:'none',
        stroke:color, 'stroke-width':2, 'marker-end':`url(#${mid})`});
      svg.appendChild(path);
      // hit-area
      const hit = el('path',{d:`M${x1} ${y1} Q ${mx} ${my} ${x2} ${y2}`, fill:'none',
        stroke:'transparent', 'stroke-width':18, 'data-aid':a.id, style:'cursor:pointer'});
      svg.appendChild(hit);
      const lt = el('text',{x:mx, y:my-6, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12, fill:color,'font-style':'italic'});
      lt.textContent = a.label; svg.appendChild(lt);
    }
    // nodes
    for(const k in OBJ){
      const o = OBJ[k];
      svg.appendChild(el('circle',{cx:o.x, cy:o.y, r:18, fill:'var(--panel2)', stroke:'var(--blue)','stroke-width':1.5}));
      const t = el('text',{x:o.x, y:o.y+5, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':14,'font-weight':600, fill:'#fff'});
      t.textContent = o.label; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // if we have a composable pair selected, sketch the 2-simplex (filled triangle)
    if(picks.length===2){
      const p = ARR.find(x=>x.id===picks[0]);
      const q = ARR.find(x=>x.id===picks[1]);
      if(p && q && p.tgt===q.src){
        const a = OBJ[p.src], b = OBJ[p.tgt], c = OBJ[q.tgt];
        svg.appendChild(el('polygon',{
          points:`${a.x},${a.y} ${b.x},${b.y} ${c.x},${c.y}`,
          fill:'color-mix(in srgb, var(--yellow) 22%, transparent)',
          stroke:'var(--yellow)', 'stroke-width':1.5,
          'stroke-dasharray':'5 4'
        }));
      }
    }
    // legend
    const lg = el('g',{transform:'translate(20, 250)'});
    const ll = el('text',{x:0,y:0,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    ll.textContent = 'objects of C, arrows = N(C)₁; pairs = N(C)₂'; lg.appendChild(ll);
    svg.appendChild(lg);
  }
  function showState(){
    if(picks.length===0){ out.textContent = 'click an arrow — it represents a 1-simplex of N(C).'; return; }
    if(picks.length===1){
      const a = ARR.find(x=>x.id===picks[0]);
      out.textContent = `1-SIMPLEX f = ${a.label} : ${a.src} → ${a.tgt}\n\n  d₀(f) = ${a.tgt} (target),  d₁(f) = ${a.src} (source).\n\nNow click a SECOND arrow to form a composable pair (its source must match this one's target).`;
      return;
    }
    const p = ARR.find(x=>x.id===picks[0]);
    const q = ARR.find(x=>x.id===picks[1]);
    if(!p || !q) return;
    if(p.tgt !== q.src){
      out.textContent = `${p.label} : ${p.src}→${p.tgt}\n${q.label} : ${q.src}→${q.tgt}\n\nNOT composable: target of first (${p.tgt}) ≠ source of second (${q.src}). Pairs (f, g) ∈ N(C)₂ require g.src = f.tgt.`;
      return;
    }
    // composable
    const compName = (p.id==='f' && q.id==='g') ? 'h = g∘f' : `${q.label} ∘ ${p.label}`;
    out.textContent =
      `2-SIMPLEX σ = (${p.label}, ${q.label}) ∈ N(C)₂\n\n` +
      `  d₀ σ = ${q.label}  (drop the first arrow)\n` +
      `  d₁ σ = ${compName}  (compose: this is the COMPOSITION face)\n` +
      `  d₂ σ = ${p.label}  (drop the last arrow)\n\n` +
      `In particular d₁(σ) is the unique morphism ${p.src} → ${q.tgt} obtained by composition — and the existence + uniqueness of this filler is exactly why N(C) is a quasi-category with unique inner-horn fillers (§6).`;
  }
  svg.addEventListener('click', (e)=>{
    const aid = e.target && e.target.getAttribute && e.target.getAttribute('data-aid');
    if(!aid) return;
    if(picks.length<2) picks.push(aid); else picks=[aid];
    draw(); showState();
  });
  reset.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ picks=[]; draw(); showState(); });
  draw(); showState();
})();
</script>

See also
  • Categories and morphisms

Used in
Simplicial sets and the nerve · Inner-horn filling and quasi-categorical nerves

5. Kan complexes

A simplicial set $X$ is a Kan complex (or satisfies the Kan extension condition) if every horn extends to a full simplex: for every $n \ge 1$, every $0 \le k \le n$, and every map of simplicial sets $\Lambda^n_k \to X$, there exists a (not necessarily unique) extension $\Delta^n \to X$:

$\begin{array}{ccc} \Lambda^n_k & \to & X \\ \downarrow & \nearrow & \\ \Delta^n & & \end{array}$

Equivalently, $X \to *$ is a Kan fibration: the unique map to the terminal object has the right lifting property against every horn inclusion $\Lambda^n_k \hookrightarrow \Delta^n$. Kan complexes are the fibrant objects in the Kan–Quillen model structure on $\mathbf{sSet}$ — the model structure that makes $|{-}| \dashv \mathrm{Sing}$ into a Quillen equivalence with $\mathbf{Top}$.

The motivating example is the singular complex $\mathrm{Sing}(Y)$ of a topological space $Y$: $|\Lambda^n_k|$ is a strong deformation retract of $|\Delta^n|$, so any continuous map $|\Lambda^n_k| \to Y$ extends to $|\Delta^n|$, and translating across the realization–singular adjunction gives the Kan condition. By Quillen's theorem, $|{-}|\dashv \mathrm{Sing}$ descends to an equivalence between the homotopy category of Kan complexes and the homotopy category of CW-complexes (or, equivalently, of all topological spaces with weak equivalences inverted).

Crucially, the nerve $N(C)$ of a category $C$ is a Kan complex iff $C$ is a groupoid. The reason is the outer horn: an outer horn $\Lambda^2_0 \to N(C)$ is a pair $f\colon a \to b$ and $h\colon a \to c$, and a filler asks for $g\colon b \to c$ with $h = g \circ f$ — this is right-divisibility, and it holds for every pair iff every morphism is invertible. Inner horns alone are easier (they correspond to ordinary composition), and they motivate §6.

params.bodyScript:
<script>
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById('svg-horns');
  const out = document.getElementById('horn-readout');
  const prev = document.getElementById('horn-prev');
  const next = document.getElementById('horn-next');
  const counter = document.getElementById('horn-counter');
  if(!svg) return;
  const NS = 'http://www.w3.org/2000/svg';
  function el(t,a){ const e=document.createElementNS(NS,t); for(const k in a) e.setAttribute(k,a[k]); return e; }

  const V = [{x:280,y:60,label:'0'},{x:120,y:240,label:'1'},{x:440,y:240,label:'2'}];
  function arrow(p, q, color){
    const dx = q.x-p.x, dy = q.y-p.y, L = Math.hypot(dx,dy);
    const ux=dx/L, uy=dy/L;
    const x1=p.x+ux*22, y1=p.y+uy*22, x2=q.x-ux*22, y2=q.y-uy*22;
    return el('line',{x1,y1,x2,y2,stroke:color,'stroke-width':3,'marker-end':'url(#arr-horn)'});
  }
  function defs(){
    const d = el('defs');
    const m = el('marker',{id:'arr-horn', viewBox:'0 0 10 10', refX:9, refY:5, markerWidth:6, markerHeight:6, orient:'auto'});
    m.appendChild(el('path',{d:'M0,0 L10,5 L0,10 z', fill:'var(--yellow)'}));
    d.appendChild(m); return d;
  }

  // Each horn omits the face OPPOSITE vertex k. So Λ²_k contains the two edges through vertex k.
  // For Λ²_0: edges (0,1) and (0,2) — both starting at 0, sharing source.
  // For Λ²_1: edges (0,1) and (1,2) — composable f then g.
  // For Λ²_2: edges (0,2) and (1,2) — both ending at 2, sharing target.
  const STATES = [
    { name:'Λ²_0 (OUTER horn)',
      edges: [[0,1],[0,2]],
      caption:
        'OUTER horn Λ²_0 — the two edges emanating from vertex 0.\n\n' +
        'Filler data needed: an edge (1,2). In a category C: given f:0→1 and h:0→2, find g:1→2 with h = g∘f.\n\n' +
        'EXTENSION HOLDS for arbitrary C iff f is right-cancellable for every target — i.e., iff C is a groupoid.\n\n' +
        '⇒ N(C) is a Kan complex iff C is a groupoid.' },
    { name:'Λ²_1 (INNER horn)',
      edges: [[0,1],[1,2]],
      caption:
        'INNER horn Λ²_1 — the two edges through vertex 1: a composable pair f:0→1, g:1→2.\n\n' +
        'Filler data needed: an edge (0,2) and a 2-simplex with d₁ = (0,2). In a category C this is just COMPOSITION g∘f — exists and is unique.\n\n' +
        '⇒ N(C) ALWAYS has unique inner-horn fillers, for any C. This is what makes N(C) a quasi-category and what §6 makes precise.' },
    { name:'Λ²_2 (OUTER horn)',
      edges: [[0,2],[1,2]],
      caption:
        'OUTER horn Λ²_2 — the two edges arriving at vertex 2: h:0→2 and g:1→2.\n\n' +
        'Filler data needed: an edge (0,1). In C: given h and g, find f:0→1 with h = g∘f.\n\n' +
        'Same story as Λ²_0 — extension iff g is left-cancellable, iff C is a groupoid.' }
  ];
  let step = 0;
  function render(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    svg.appendChild(defs());
    const s = STATES[step];
    // outline of full Δ² in muted dashed
    svg.appendChild(el('polygon',{points:V.map(v=>`${v.x},${v.y}`).join(' '),
      fill:'transparent', stroke:'var(--mute)', 'stroke-width':1, 'stroke-dasharray':'4 4'}));
    // present edges
    for(const [a,b] of s.edges){
      svg.appendChild(arrow(V[a], V[b], 'var(--yellow)'));
    }
    // vertices
    for(const v of V){
      svg.appendChild(el('circle',{cx:v.x, cy:v.y, r:14, fill:'var(--panel2)', stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':1.5}));
      const t = el('text',{x:v.x, y:v.y+5, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,'font-weight':600, fill:'#fff'});
      t.textContent = v.label; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // missing-edge label in faded
    const all = [[0,1],[0,2],[1,2]];
    const present = new Set(s.edges.map(e=>e.join(',')));
    for(const [a,b] of all){
      if(present.has(`${a},${b}`)) continue;
      const A = V[a], B = V[b];
      const mx=(A.x+B.x)/2, my=(A.y+B.y)/2;

  next.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ if(step<STATES.length-1){ step++; render(); }});
  render();
})();
</script>

<script>
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById('svg-horns');
  const out = document.getElementById('horn-readout');
  const prev = document.getElementById('horn-prev');
  const next = document.getElementById('horn-next');
  const counter = document.getElementById('horn-counter');
  if(!svg) return;
  const NS = 'http://www.w3.org/2000/svg';
  function el(t,a){ const e=document.createElementNS(NS,t); for(const k in a) e.setAttribute(k,a[k]); return e; }

  const V = [{x:280,y:60,label:'0'},{x:120,y:240,label:'1'},{x:440,y:240,label:'2'}];
  function arrow(p, q, color){
    const dx = q.x-p.x, dy = q.y-p.y, L = Math.hypot(dx,dy);
    const ux=dx/L, uy=dy/L;
    const x1=p.x+ux*22, y1=p.y+uy*22, x2=q.x-ux*22, y2=q.y-uy*22;
    return el('line',{x1,y1,x2,y2,stroke:color,'stroke-width':3,'marker-end':'url(#arr-horn)'});
  }
  function defs(){
    const d = el('defs');
    const m = el('marker',{id:'arr-horn', viewBox:'0 0 10 10', refX:9, refY:5, markerWidth:6, markerHeight:6, orient:'auto'});
    m.appendChild(el('path',{d:'M0,0 L10,5 L0,10 z', fill:'var(--yellow)'}));
    d.appendChild(m); return d;
  }

  // Each horn omits the face OPPOSITE vertex k. So Λ²_k contains the two edges through vertex k.
  // For Λ²_0: edges (0,1) and (0,2) — both starting at 0, sharing source.
  // For Λ²_1: edges (0,1) and (1,2) — composable f then g.
  // For Λ²_2: edges (0,2) and (1,2) — both ending at 2, sharing target.
  const STATES = [
    { name:'Λ²_0 (OUTER horn)',
      edges: [[0,1],[0,2]],
      caption:
        'OUTER horn Λ²_0 — the two edges emanating from vertex 0.\n\n' +
        'Filler data needed: an edge (1,2). In a category C: given f:0→1 and h:0→2, find g:1→2 with h = g∘f.\n\n' +
        'EXTENSION HOLDS for arbitrary C iff f is right-cancellable for every target — i.e., iff C is a groupoid.\n\n' +
        '⇒ N(C) is a Kan complex iff C is a groupoid.' },
    { name:'Λ²_1 (INNER horn)',
      edges: [[0,1],[1,2]],
      caption:
        'INNER horn Λ²_1 — the two edges through vertex 1: a composable pair f:0→1, g:1→2.\n\n' +
        'Filler data needed: an edge (0,2) and a 2-simplex with d₁ = (0,2). In a category C this is just COMPOSITION g∘f — exists and is unique.\n\n' +
        '⇒ N(C) ALWAYS has unique inner-horn fillers, for any C. This is what makes N(C) a quasi-category and what §6 makes precise.' },
    { name:'Λ²_2 (OUTER horn)',
      edges: [[0,2],[1,2]],
      caption:
        'OUTER horn Λ²_2 — the two edges arriving at vertex 2: h:0→2 and g:1→2.\n\n' +
        'Filler data needed: an edge (0,1). In C: given h and g, find f:0→1 with h = g∘f.\n\n' +
        'Same story as Λ²_0 — extension iff g is left-cancellable, iff C is a groupoid.' }
  ];
  let step = 0;
  function render(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    svg.appendChild(defs());
    const s = STATES[step];
    // outline of full Δ² in muted dashed
    svg.appendChild(el('polygon',{points:V.map(v=>`${v.x},${v.y}`).join(' '),
      fill:'transparent', stroke:'var(--mute)', 'stroke-width':1, 'stroke-dasharray':'4 4'}));

    for(const [a,b] of s.edges){
      svg.appendChild(arrow(V[a], V[b], 'var(--yellow)'));

    for(const v of V){
      svg.appendChild(el('circle',{cx:v.x, cy:v.y, r:14, fill:'var(--panel2)', stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':1.5}));
      const t = el('text',{x:v.x, y:v.y+5, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,'font-weight':600, fill:'#fff'});
      t.textContent = v.label; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // missing-edge label in faded
    const all = [[0,1],[0,2],[1,2]];
    const present = new Set(s.edges.map(e=>e.join(',')));
    for(const [a,b] of all){
      if(present.has(`${a},${b}`)) continue;
      const A = V[a], B = V[b];
      const mx=(A.x+B.x)/2, my=(A.y+B.y)/2;
      const t = el('text',{x:mx, y:my-4, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11, fill:'var(--mute)','font-style':'italic'});
      t.textContent = `(${a},${b}) — missing`; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    const ttl = el('text',{x:280, y:24, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':14, fill:'var(--ink)','font-weight':600});
    ttl.textContent = s.name; svg.appendChild(ttl);
    counter.textContent = `${step+1} / ${STATES.length}`;
    out.textContent = s.caption;
    prev.disabled = step===0;
    next.disabled = step===STATES.length-1;
  }
  prev.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ if(step>0){ step--; render(); }});
  next.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ if(step<STATES.length-1){ step++; render(); }});
  render();
})();
</script>

Simplicial sets and the nerve · Inner-horn filling and quasi-categorical nerves
Type theory and HoTT · Models of HoTT

6. Inner-horn filling and quasi-categorical nerves

The Kan condition asks for fillers of every horn, including the outer ones $\Lambda^n_0$ and $\Lambda^n_n$ that encode invertibility. Demanding fillers only for inner horns ($0 < k < n$) gives a strictly weaker condition that nevertheless captures all the algebra of composition. A simplicial set in which every inner horn extends is called a quasi-category (or weak Kan complex), and these are the fibrant objects of the Joyal model structure on $\mathbf{sSet}$.

Quasi-categories are Boardman–Vogt and Joyal–Lurie's model for $(\infty, 1)$-categories. The picture: $0$-simplices are objects, $1$-simplices are morphisms, $2$-simplices witness compositions ($d_1$ is "the composite of $d_0$ and $d_2$", but the composite is determined only up to homotopy, giving an entire space of composites), and higher simplices encode coherences between coherences ad infinitum. Inner-horn filling is exactly the assertion "every composable string admits a composition".

The fundamental theorem of nerves: $X = N(C)$ for some small category $C$ iff $X$ is a quasi-category in which every inner horn has a unique filler. Existence of fillers is "$X$ has compositions"; uniqueness is "the composition is a function, not a higher structure" — i.e., $X$ is a $1$-category. This is the precise sense in which classical category theory is the "$1$-truncated" piece of $\infty$-category theory.

Two further refinements. (a) The Kan complexes (all horns fill, possibly non-uniquely) sit inside quasi-categories as those with all morphisms invertible — the $\infty$-groupoids. (b) For an arbitrary quasi-category $\mathcal{C}$, the homotopy category $h\mathcal{C}$ is obtained by quotienting $\mathcal{C}_1$ by homotopy of $1$-simplices; this $h\mathcal{C}$ is an honest $1$-category. The functor $h \dashv N$ is a Quillen pair between the Joyal model on $\mathbf{sSet}$ and $\mathbf{Cat}$, restricting to the inclusion $\mathbf{Cat} \hookrightarrow \mathbf{sSet}$ on the nerve side.

params.bodyScript:
<script>
(function(){
  const svg = document.getElementById('svg-inner-horn');
  const out = document.getElementById('ih-readout');
  const fillBtn = document.getElementById('ih-fill');
  const reset = document.getElementById('ih-reset');
  if(!svg) return;
  const NS = 'http://www.w3.org/2000/svg';
  function el(t,a){ const e=document.createElementNS(NS,t); for(const k in a) e.setAttribute(k,a[k]); return e; }

  let filled = false;
  const V = [{x:120,y:200,label:'a'},{x:280,y:60,label:'b'},{x:440,y:200,label:'c'}];

  function defs(){
    const d = el('defs');
    for(const c of ['yellow','green','mute']){
      const m = el('marker',{id:'arr-ih-'+c, viewBox:'0 0 10 10', refX:9, refY:5, markerWidth:6, markerHeight:6, orient:'auto'});
      m.appendChild(el('path',{d:'M0,0 L10,5 L0,10 z', fill:`var(--${c})`}));
      d.appendChild(m);
    }
    return d;
  }
  function arrow(p, q, color, mid, label, lyShift){
    const dx = q.x-p.x, dy = q.y-p.y, L = Math.hypot(dx,dy);
    const ux=dx/L, uy=dy/L;
    const x1=p.x+ux*22, y1=p.y+uy*22, x2=q.x-ux*22, y2=q.y-uy*22;
    const path = el('line',{x1,y1,x2,y2,stroke:color,'stroke-width':2.5,'marker-end':`url(#arr-ih-${mid})`});
    const mx=(x1+x2)/2, my=(y1+y2)/2;
    const lt = el('text',{x:mx, y:my+(lyShift||-8), 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,
      fill:color,'font-style':'italic'});
    lt.textContent = label;
    return [path, lt];
  }

  function draw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    svg.appendChild(defs());
    if(filled){
      // shaded 2-simplex
      svg.appendChild(el('polygon',{points:V.map(v=>`${v.x},${v.y}`).join(' '),
        fill:'color-mix(in srgb, var(--green) 22%, transparent)',
        stroke:'var(--green)', 'stroke-width':1.5}));
    }
    // f: a→b
    arrow(V[0], V[1], 'var(--yellow)', 'yellow', 'f').forEach(n=>svg.appendChild(n));
    // g: b→c
    arrow(V[1], V[2], 'var(--yellow)', 'yellow', 'g').forEach(n=>svg.appendChild(n));
    // h = g∘f: a→c (only if filled)
    if(filled){
      arrow(V[0], V[2], 'var(--green)', 'green', 'h = g∘f', 16).forEach(n=>svg.appendChild(n));
    } else {
      // ghost dashed missing edge
      const a=V[0], b=V[2];
      const dx = b.x-a.x, dy = b.y-a.y, L = Math.hypot(dx,dy);
      const ux=dx/L, uy=dy/L;
      const x1=a.x+ux*22, y1=a.y+uy*22, x2=b.x-ux*22, y2=b.y-uy*22;
      svg.appendChild(el('line',{x1,y1,x2,y2, stroke:'var(--mute)','stroke-width':1.5,
        'stroke-dasharray':'5 4', 'marker-end':'url(#arr-ih-mute)'}));
      const mx=(x1+x2)/2, my=(y1+y2)/2;
      const lt = el('text',{x:mx, y:my+16, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12, fill:'var(--mute)','font-style':'italic'});
      lt.textContent = 'd₁σ — needed'; svg.appendChild(lt);
    }
    // nodes
    for(const v of V){
      svg.appendChild(el('circle',{cx:v.x, cy:v.y, r:18, fill:'var(--panel2)', stroke:'var(--blue)','stroke-width':1.5}));
      const t = el('text',{x:v.x, y:v.y+5, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':14,'font-weight':600, fill:'#fff'});
      t.textContent = v.label; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    const ttl = el('text',{x:280,y:22,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':13,
      fill: filled ? 'var(--green)' : 'var(--yellow)', 'font-weight':600});
    ttl.textContent = filled ? 'σ ∈ N(C)₂ — the unique filler' : 'Λ²₁ → N(C): inner horn given by (f, g)';
    svg.appendChild(ttl);
  }
  function showState(){
    if(!filled){
      out.textContent =
        'STATE: an inner horn Λ²₁ → N(C) consisting of f : a→b and g : b→c.\n\n' +
        'To extend to a 2-simplex Δ² → N(C) we need (i) the third edge d₁σ : a→c and (ii) the 2-simplex σ itself.\n\n' +
        'Click "fill the horn" to invoke composition.';
    } else {
      out.textContent =
        'FILLED: σ = (f, g) ∈ N(C)₂.\n\n' +
        '  d₂σ = f, d₀σ = g, d₁σ = g∘f.\n\n' +
        'Existence of σ ⇔ (f, g) is composable in C — always true in a 1-category. UNIQUENESS of σ ⇔ composition is a function, not just defined up to homotopy. Both hold for N(C); only existence holds in a general quasi-category.\n\n' +
        'CHARACTERISATION: a quasi-category X is the nerve of a 1-category iff every inner-horn filler is unique.';
    }
  }
  fillBtn.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ filled=true; draw(); showState(); });
  reset.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ filled=false; draw(); showState(); });
  draw(); showState();
})();
</script>
